title: What to do in a mental health crisis
citation: World Health Organization (2013). Mental Health Action Plan 2013-2020. WHO.
content:
A mental health crisis is any situation in which a person's thoughts, feelings, or behaviour put them at risk of harming themselves or others, or in which they are so severely distressed or disoriented that they cannot care for themselves. Crises can develop gradually or arise suddenly. Recognising one early makes it possible to get help before the situation becomes more dangerous.

If you are with someone who appears to be in crisis, the most important thing is to stay with them. Do not leave them alone. Try to remain calm and speak in a steady, reassuring voice. Listen without judging. You do not need to have the right words. Your presence and attention matter more than what you say. Do not promise to keep what they tell you secret, particularly if they are talking about harming themselves, because getting them safe may require involving others.

If there is immediate risk of harm, remove access to any means of self-harm if it is safe to do so. Call for emergency help. In the UAE, you can call the National Crisis and Emergency Line on 920003, or take the person to the nearest emergency department. SEHA's mental health services are reachable on 800-7342.

For the person in crisis: what you are feeling right now is not permanent, even when it feels that way. You do not have to manage this alone. Help exists and it is available to you. Many people who have been in the place you are in now have found their way through with the right support. Reaching out is the most important step.

Recovery is possible. Crisis is not the end of the story.
